debug_info.goal:
q ε : ℝ
E : StaticElectricField 1
⊢ E.GaussLaw ε Electromagnetism.OneDimVacuum.chargeDistribution ↔ ∃ m, E = constD 1 m

failed_attempts:
rw [StaticElectricField.gaussLaw_iff]
  constructor
  · intro h
    have h0 : divD E = 0 := by
      have : (1 / ε) • (0 : 𝓢(Space 1, ℝ) →L[ℝ] ℝ) = 0 := by simp
      rwa [show (chargeDistribution : ChargeDistribution 1) =
        (0 : 𝓢(Space 1, ℝ) →L[ℝ] ℝ) from rfl, this] at h
    /- In 1D, divD E = 0 means the distributional derivative of E is zero.
       By the fundamental theorem of distribution theory, this implies E is constant.
       This requires: (1) any Schwartz function with zero integral is a derivative of a
       Schwartz function, (2) decomposition of test functions. -/
    sorry
  · rintro ⟨m, rfl⟩
    show divD (constD 1 m) = _
    rw [divD_constD]
    show (0 : 𝓢(Space 1, ℝ) →L[ℝ] ℝ) = _
    rw [show (chargeDistribution : ChargeDistribution 1) =
      (0 : 𝓢(Space 1, ℝ) →L[ℝ] ℝ) from rfl]
    simp
